marketState: neutral with bullish short-term skew — historically extreme heat demand above 5y max supports near-term price while storage surplus and bearish 4-week seasonality cap medium-term upside
summary: Aug 2026 NG=F trades at $3.335, sitting ~$0.375 above model fair value ($2.960) but still within the $2.460–$3.460 fair band; the overvaluation is mild and self-correcting since the 21-day forward fair rises to $3.189, meaning the current weather premium converges toward fair without requiring a selloff. The dominant near-term bull signal is weather: 1–7D demand is 29% above the 5y average and already above the 5y maximum range, while the 8–16D window is even more extreme at +56% above 5y average and also above the 5y maximum — a back-to-back above-max condition that is historically rare; the 24-hour trend surged +20.9 to stand 45% above the 5y normal. Against this, 4-week seasonal probabilities are firmly bearish (only 19% historical win rate, typical –1.8% move, reliability rated LOW with 51 pp spread), storage at +151 Bcf above the 5y average arrived with a +6 Bcf bearish surprise, and both the daily and weekly EMA structures are bearish (50-day below 200-day). Managed-money net positioning at the 17th 5-year percentile (net short –84,909) is the key contrarian wildcard: it creates squeeze risk if weather sustains but is likely to unwind once heat abates.
